content:
In the feedback control system shown in the figure below $G(s) = \dfrac{6}{s(s+1)(s+2)}$.

$R(s), Y(s),$ and $E(s)$ are the Laplace transforms of $r(t), y(t),$ and $e(t)$, respectively. If the input $r(t)$ is a unit step function, then __________

options.content: $\lim\limits_{t \to \infty} e(t)$ does not exist, $e(t)$ is oscillatory

explanation:
Given : The open loop transfer function is given by,

$$ G(s)=\frac{6}{s(s+1)(s+2)} $$

$$ E(s)=R(s)-Y(s) H(s) $$

For Negative unity feedback system :

$$ \begin{aligned} & H(s)=1 \\ & E(s)=R(s)-Y(s) \\ & E(s)=R(s)-E(s) G(s) \\ & E(s)[1+G(s)]=R(s) \\ & E(s)=\frac{R(s)}{1+G(s)} \end{aligned} $$

As we know that from final value theorem :

$$ e_{s s}=\lim _{t \rightarrow \infty} e(t)=\lim _{s \rightarrow 0} s E(s) $$

$$ \begin{aligned} & \lim _{t \rightarrow \infty} e(t)=\lim _{s \rightarrow 0} \frac{s R(s)}{1+G(s)} \\ & \lim _{t \rightarrow \infty} e(t)=\lim _{s \rightarrow 0} \frac{s \times \frac{1}{s}}{1+\frac{6}{s(s+1)(s+2)}}=0 \end{aligned} $$

$\Rightarrow \quad$ This is wrong. First we need to check whether the system is stable or not.

$\Rightarrow \quad$ To check the stability of a closed loop system. We use Routh's stability criterion.

The characteristic equation is,

$$ \begin{aligned} & 1+G(s)=0 \\ & 1+\frac{6}{s(s+1)(s+2)}=0 \\ & \left(s^2+s\right)(s+2)+6=0 \end{aligned} $$

Internal Product (IP) $=3 \times 2=6$

External Product $(\mathrm{EP})=6$

$\mathrm{IP}=\mathrm{EP}$ : System is marginal stable, i.e., $\xi=0$.

Note :

(i) It is meaningless to find the steady state errors for unstable (or) marginal closed loop system. So we have to calculate the steady state errors only for closed loop stable systems.

(ii) This means we need to check whether the control system is stable or not before finding the steady state errors.

Hence, the correct option is (D).

content: The step error coefficient of a system G(s)=$$\frac1{\left(s+6\right)\left(s+1\right)}$$ with unity feedback is
The poles of a continuous time oscillator are ___________.

Consider a unity negative feedback control system with forward path gain $G(s) = \frac{K}{(s + 1)(s + 2)(s + 3)}$ as shown.

The impulse response of the closed-loop system decays faster than $e^{-t}$ if ________.
explanation:
$$ \text { Given : } G(s)=\frac{K}{(s+1)(s+2)(s+3)} $$

The characteristic equation is given by

$$ \begin{aligned} & 1+G(s)=0 \\ & 1+\frac{K}{(s+1)(s+2)(s+3)}=0 \\ & (s+1)(s+2)(s+3)+K=0 \,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,...(i)\end{aligned} $$

For the impulse response of the closed loop system to decay faster than $e^{-t}$ at the poles must lies on the left side of $s=-1$. Hence, replacing $S$ by $z-1$ in the characteristic equation

Substituting $s=z-1$ in equation (i)

New characteristic equation is,

$$ \begin{gathered} (z-1+1)(z-1+2)(z-1+3)+K=0 \\ z(z+1)(z+2)+K=0 \\ z^3+3 z^2+2 z+K=0 \end{gathered} $$

$$ \begin{array}{c|cc} \hline z^3 & 1 & 2 \\ z^2 & 3 & K \\ z^1 & \frac{6-K}{3} & 0 \\ z^0 & K & 0 \end{array} $$

For the system to be stable

$$ \text { and } \quad \begin{aligned} & K>0 \\ & \frac{6-K}{3}>0 \\ & 6>K \end{aligned} $$

On combining the above two conditions.

$$ 0

From the options option (A) lies in the above range of $K$.

Hence, the correct option is (A).

content: A closed loop system is shown in the figure where $$k>0$$ and $$\alpha > 0$$. The steady state error due to a ramp input $$(R(s) = \alpha /{s^2})$$ is given by
explanation:
Given :

$$ R(s)=\frac{\alpha}{s^2} $$

$$ G(s)=\frac{K}{s(s+2)} $$

Steady state error for ramp input is given by

$$ \begin{aligned} e_{s s} & =\frac{\alpha}{K_v} \\ K_v & =\lim _{s \rightarrow 0} s G(s)=\lim _{s \rightarrow 0} \frac{K}{(s+2)} \\ K_v & =\frac{K}{2} \\ e_{s s} & =\frac{\alpha}{K / 2} \\ e_{s s} & =\frac{2 \alpha}{K} \end{aligned} $$

Hence, the correct option is (A).

content:
Two linear time-invariant systems with transfer functions

$${G_1}(s) = {{10} \over {{s^2} + s + 1}}$$ and $${G_2}(s) = {{10} \over {{s^2} + s\sqrt {10} + 10}}$$

have unit step responses y1(t) and y2(t), respectively. Which of the following statements is/are true?

options.content: y<sub>1</sub>(t) and y<sub>2</sub>(t) have the same percentage peak overshoot.
y<sub>1</sub>(t) and y<sub>2</sub>(t) have the same steady-state value.
y<sub>1</sub>(t) and y<sub>2</sub>(t) have the same damped frequency of oscillation.
y<sub>1</sub>(t) and y<sub>2</sub>(t) have the same 2% settling time.

explanation:
For system $${G_1}(s) = {{10} \over {{s^2} + s + 1}}$$

Characteristics equation,

$${s^2} + s + 1 = 0$$

The standard characteristics equation is

$${s^2} + 2\xi {\omega _n}s + \omega _n^2 = 0$$

On comparing, $${\omega _n} = 1$$, $$\xi = {1 \over 2} = 0.5$$

$${\omega _d} = {\omega _n}\sqrt {1 - {\xi ^2}} = 1\sqrt {1 - {{(0.5)}^2}} = 0.866$$

Settling time, $${t_s} = {4 \over {\xi {\omega _n}}} = 8$$ sec

Steady-state error,

$${e_{ss}} = \mathop {\lim }\limits_{s \to 0} {{s\left( {{1 \over s}} \right)\,.\,10} \over {{s^2} + s + 1}} = 10$$

$${e_{ss}} = 10$$

For system, $${G_2}(s) = {{10} \over {{s^2} + \sqrt {10} s + 10}}$$

Characteristics equation,

$${s^2} + \sqrt {10} s + 10 = 0$$

Standard characteristics equation,

$${s^2} + 2\xi {\omega _n}s + \omega _n^2 = 0$$

On comparing, $$\omega _n^2 = 10 \Rightarrow {\omega _n} = \sqrt {10} $$

$$2\xi {\omega _n} = \sqrt {10} \Rightarrow \xi = 0.5$$

$${\omega _d} = {\omega _n}\sqrt {1 - {\xi ^2}} = \sqrt {10} \sqrt {1 - {{(0.5)}^2}} = 2.739$$

Settling time, $${t_s} = {4 \over {\xi {\omega _n}}} = {4 \over {0.5\sqrt {10} }} = {8 \over {\sqrt {10} }} = 2.535$$

Steady state error, $${e_{ss}} = \mathop {\lim }\limits_{s \to 0} {{s\left( {{1 \over s}} \right)\,.\,10} \over {{s^2} + \sqrt {10} s + 10}} = 1$$

$${e_{ss}} = 1$$

Since '$$\xi $$' value for both the system is same. So percentage peak overshoot for both system is same.

content: The block diagram of a closed-loop control system is shown in the figure. R(s), Y(s), and D(s) are the Laplace transforms of the time-domain signals r(t), y(t), and d(t), respectively. Let the error signal be defined as e(t) = r(t) $$-$$ y(t). Assuming the reference input r(t) = 0 for all t, the steady-state error e($$\infty$$), due to a unit step disturbance d(t), is __________ (rounded off to two decimal places).
explanation:
G1(s) = 10, G2(s) = $${1 \over {s(s + 10)}}$$

$${{E(s)} \over {D(s)}} = {{ - {G_2}(s)} \over {1 + {G_1}(s){G_2}(s)}}$$

$$ = {{{{ - 1} \over {s(s + 10)}}} \over {1 + \left( {10 \times {1 \over {s(s + 10)}}} \right)}}$$

$$ = {1 \over {{s^2} + 10s + 10}}$$

$${e_{ss}} = \mathop {\lim }\limits_{t \to \infty } e(t) = \mathop {\lim }\limits_{s \to 0} sE(s)$$

$${e_{ss}} = \mathop {\lim }\limits_{s \to 0} {{s(1/s)( - 1)} \over {{s^2} + 10s + 10}}$$

$${e_{ss}} = {{ - 1} \over {10}}$$

$${e_{ss}} = - 0.1$$

content:
The block diagram of a feedback system is shown in Figure.
(a) Find the closed loop transfer function.
(b) Find the minimum value of G for which the step response of the system would exhibit an overshoot, as shown in Figure.
(c) For G equal to twice this minimum value, find the time period T indicated in Figure.
answer:
(a) Closed loop transfer function= $$\frac G{s^2+3s+G}$$
(b) G = 6.25
(c) T= 1.96 sec

content:
Following fig. shows the block diagram representation of control system. The system in block A has an impulse response h(t ) = e−t u(t ). The system in block B has an impulse response h(t ) = e−2t u(t ). The block 'K' amplifies its inputs by a factor k. For the overall system with input x(t) and output y(t). (a) Find the transfer function $$\frac{y\left(s\right)}{x\left(s\right)}$$ when k =1.
(b) Find the impulse response when k = 0.
(c) Find the values of k for which the system becomes unstable.
answer:
(a) $$\frac{y\left(s\right)}{x\left(s\right)}=\frac1{s^2+3s+3}$$
(b) Impulse response h(t)=$$\left(e^{-t}-e^{-2t}\right)u\left(t\right)$$
(c) When k<-2 system is unstable.
